response.justification: Strong senior engineer with directly relevant geospatial domain expertise and proven systems-level thinking, but faces a significant blocker: no professional C++ work since 2015 (11-year gap), despite it being the primary required skill. Recent years show a shift toward TypeScript/Node.js. However, D language experience demonstrates recent systems programming capability and the candidate's track record of rapidly learning new languages suggests C++ could be refreshable with sufficient motivation.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and multiple GIS projects directly applicable to routing data pipelines
Proven experience architecting distributed systems at massive scale (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency optimization)
Recent systems programming capability via D language and demonstrated ability to master new technical domains quickly
concerns: Eleven-year gap since last professional C++ work (ended 2015); primary required skill has not been maintained despite being core to the role
No demonstrated background in navigation guidance systems, routing algorithms, or tile-based data transformation pipelines
Recent career trajectory (2021-present) focuses entirely on TypeScript/JavaScript, creating risk of skill drift from systems-level programming

key_factors.factor: C++ skill currency and maintenance gap
